arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta is backing Arsenal pre-match change to North London Forever and player name calls
arts.sum: Fans who have been privileged enough to be inside the Emirates Stadium in the last few weeks might have noticed some changes to the pre-match and in-game sounds coming through the speakers. The words have become more visceral and hearty of late, not that it wasn't energetic before, but there is certainly a real oomph to it alongside a reminder to fans to get in their seats and "Bring the noise!" This is repeated before the second half starts as well, where supporters are still making their way back to their seats.

arts.ttl: Arsenal drop early Gabriel Martinelli team news hint for Everton as double clue emerges
arts.sum: Arsenal's pre-match social media activity on Saturday morning may have dropped a huge hint over who Mikel Arteta may start against Everton. Posting online, Arsenal dropped an image of Gabriel Martinelli and Jurrien Timber as their pre-match graphic.

arts.ttl: Arsenal vs Everton Premier League score predicted as huge title race moment takes place
arts.sum: Arsenal can move to second in the Premier League later today when the Gunners welcome Everton to the Emirates Stadium. But, with Arsenal playing before Chelsea, the Gunners could jump to second in the Premier League table later today, albeit it may only be for just over a day.

arts.ttl: Arsenal vs Everton LIVE: Kick-off time, TV channel, team news, live stream details
arts.sum: Arsenal take on Everton at the Emirates Stadium looking to return to winning ways in the Premier League following the disappointing draw at Fulham last weekend. Everton could see Michael Keane return while a host of other injured players were involved in the Toffees’ youth team match this week.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta explains lack of minutes for Raheem Sterling after Chelsea loan
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta explains Raheem Sterling's lack of minutes at Arsenal following his loan from Chelsea.

arts.ttl: Manager names “ideal person” for Arsenal transfer – Could elevate set pieces further
arts.sum: Club Brugge defender Maxim De Cuyper would be an ideal signing for Arsenal and could elevate their set piece routines even further. That’s according to Belgian boss Hein Vanhaezebrouck, via Voetbal 24, who believes Arsenal should move for the 23-year-old if they ever decide to sign a left-back.
